data.title: Upbeat BidCo Increases Offer Price for French Digital Music Company Believe
data.description:
Upbeat BidCo increased its offer price for Believe to 17.20 euros per share from 15.30 euros for its remaining minority shareholders.

French digital music company Believe's board said the offer is in the interests of minority shareholders by offering immediate liquidity, according to a Wednesday release. The board also received a fairness opinion from Finexsi - Expert & Conseil Financier.

data.title: Cube's All-Store Sales Surge 11% in May
data.description:
Cube's all-store sales increased 11% in May, bringing its cumulative sales growth to 5.4% year on year, according to its Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The company's e-commerce sales for all stores recorded a 1.1% decline, while physical store sales jumped 18% on year in May.

Year to date, all stores' e-commerce sales fell 9% but physical stores gained 13.9%.

For existing stores, May sales rose 4.9% while year-to-date sales slipped 0.8%.

data.title: India's Honasa Consumer rises after tribunal approves merger with subsidiaries
data.description:
** Beauty and personal care company Honasa Consumer up as much as 3.4% to 329.5 rupees, last up ~1%

** Mamaearth-parent gets National Company Law Tribunal approval to merge with its subsidiaries Fusion Cosmeceutics and Just4Kids Services

** Fusion retails the Dr. Sheth's skin care brand

** Merger to prevent cost duplication, improve financial efficiency and co's competitive position, says Honasa

** With session's gains, HONA shares up ~26% YTD

data.description:

INTERNATIONAL/REGIONAL

data.title: Australia 10-Year Bond Yield Edges Higher
data.description:
Australia’s 10-year government bond yield edged up to 4.24% on Thursday, despite weaker-than-expected trade data.

The country's trade surplus in goods narrowed to AUD 5.41 billion in April, from a marginally revised AUD 6.89 billion in March and below market forecasts of AUD 5.90 billion.

The softer surplus was primarily driven by a 2.4% monthly decline in exports, weighed down by sluggish overseas demand, while imports increased by 1.1%.

Meanwhile, data released earlier this week showed that the Australian economy expanded by 1.3% in the first quarter, matching the previous quarter’s pace but falling short of the anticipated 1.5%.

The subdued growth—attributed to weaker public spending, consumer demand, and exports—has reinforced expectations of further monetary easing by the Reserve Bank of Australia.

Markets are now implying an 82% probability of a rate cut in July, up from 77% prior to the data release.

data.title: Fitch Keeps Société Générale's Rating on Sound Capitalization, Diversified Business Profile
data.description:
Fitch on Wednesday affirmed the long-term issuer default rating of Société Générale at A-, with a stable outlook.

The rating affirmation highlights the lender's sound capitalization, diversified business profile, and moderate risk profile.

The rating agency also noted the bank's moderate asset-quality risks, an improvement in its profitability, and a diversified funding base.

data.title: India long duration bonds rise on bets of dovish RBI policy
data.description:
Indian government bonds rose in early deals on Thursday, led by longer-duration papers, as traders added positions a day before the central bank is widely expected to deliver a policy rate cut.

The yield on the benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was at 6.1966% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with the previous close of 6.2065%. The five-year 6.75% 2029 bond yield (IN067529G=CC) was at 5.8461% after ending at 5.8514%.

Bond yields move inversely to prices.

"Bond prices could rally if there is something more apart from a rate cut, which is leading to some long build-up. Also, Treasury yields have come down and that is having a sentimental impact," a trader with a primary dealership says.

The Reserve Bank of India's policy decision is due on Friday when it is widely expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points for a third consecutive time this year.

Muted inflation provides ample space to focus on boosting economic growth further, traders said.

The RBI has lowered rates by 50 bps in 2025 and has infused $100 billion into the banking system in the December-May period.

While most expect a 25-bp cut, the State Bank of India said the RBI should slash rates by 50 bps to jumpstart the credit cycle.

DBS is expecting a 25-bp cut on Friday, followed by another similar cut in July-December.

Some traders expect the RBI to undertake more liquidity-boosting measures, which will further support short-end bond prices.

RATES

There was not much activity in the overnight index swap (OIS) market. The one-year (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) OIS rate and two-year OIS rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) were not yet traded after ending at 5.54%, and 5.43% respectively.

However, the most liquid five-year (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) OIS was seeing some receiving interest and was down to 5.62%, in the key technical support zone.

data.title: Dyaco International to Buy Back Shares Worth Up to NT$2.5 Billion
data.description:
Dyaco International's board approved a plan to repurchase up to 4 million common shares for up to NT$2.5 billion.

The shares will be bought back at between NT$20 and NT$34 apiece, from June 5 to July 4, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: CATL Ties Up With AP Moller-Maersk's Independent Division
data.description:
Contemporary Amperex Technology or CATL partnered with APM Terminals, an independent division of Danish shipping and logistics firm AP Moller-Maersk, for the energy transition of the global logistics industry, the Chinese largest battery maker said on its webpage on Wednesday.

CATL will provide high-performance batteries for APM Terminals' container handling equipment, including electric terminal tractors, and will share best practices for reducing greenhouse gas emissions associated with battery handling.

data.title: Elegance Optical Weighs Fundraising for Liquor and Jewellery Push; Shares Slide 5%
data.description:
Elegance Optical said it may raise funds to support new business plans, adding that options include a possible rights issue, according to a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The group's unit, DongChuang Xintou, plans to expand into liquor, food seasonings, optical products, and jewellery. It also aims to set up several subsidiaries in China for these businesses, the filing said.

Shares of the optical manufacturer were down about 5% in recent trade.

data.title: KSH Establishes Step Down Subsidiary
data.description:
KSH subsidiary, Kim Seng Heng Realty, established a step down subsidiary, KSH Quantum, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The principal activity of the subsidiary is property development.

data.title: Warehouse REIT to Buy British Industrial Estate for GBP35 Million
data.description:
Warehouse REIT agreed to purchase the Rycote Lane multi-let industrial estate in Thame, England, for 34.8 million pounds sterling.

The acquisition, which includes 14 units offering a contracted rent of 2.1 million pounds, is anticipated to be finalized at the end of September, the real estate company said Wednesday.

data.title: Dongfeng Motor Shares Slide After Restructuring U-Turn
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Shares in Dongfeng Motor plunged after the disclosure that the Chinese auto giant isn't currently engaged in any business restructuring, coming just months after it announced plans for one.

The carmaker's stock was 13% lower at 3.68 Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to 47 U.S. cents, by midday in Hong Kong, back near levels of early February, before shares surged on news of a potential restructuring. Dongfeng's Shanghai-listed shares slumped 6.9%.

The company, a manufacturing partner of Chrysler parent Stellantis, said in a Thursday filing that its controlling shareholder isn't involved in any restructuring at the moment. The company's normal production and operations wouldn't be affected, Dongfeng cited the shareholder as saying.

The Wuhan, China-based company in February said it was planning a restructuring with another state-owned enterprise. Dongfeng had said that while it might get a new controlling shareholder, that won't lead to a change in its "actual controller," a term referring to the person or entity with ultimate control over a company, which in the case of state-owned enterprises is often the government.

The statement back then had fueled speculation among investors of a potential merger between Dongfeng Motor and Chongqing Changan Automobile, which rank among China's "big four" state-run automakers.

Changan Automobile and Dongfeng didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

Competition in China's auto industry has intensified this year after electric-vehicle leader BYD offered steep discounts on a range of models recently, prompting some rivals to follow suit.

The China Passenger Car Association estimated that sales of electric and hybrid passenger cars rose 38% in May from a year earlier to about 1.24 million units.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Burckhardt Compression continues to deliver strong growth and increased profitability
data.description:
Ad hoc announcement pursuant to Art. 53 LR of June 5th, 2025  

Winterthur, June 5th, 2025

  • Burckhardt Compression achieved the following record results in fiscal year 2024:
  • Sales of CHF 1'095.6 mn, +12.6% year-on-year
  • Operating income (EBIT) of CHF 140.8 mn, +23.2% year-on-year
  • Earnings per share of CHF 31.20, +24.9% year-on-year
  • Book-to-bill ratio above 1, with order intake of CHF 1'151.2 mn (+2.4% year-on-year)  
  • Profitability increased in both divisions
  • Operating cash flow of CHF 212.8 mn
  • Dividend of CHF 18.00 proposed, an increase of 16.1% year-on-year
  • 40.0% reduction in the Group's greenhouse gas emission intensity (Scope 1 and 2)
  • Upcoming changes in the Board of Directors
  • Guidance for fiscal year 2025: sales and EBIT margin at a similar level as in fiscal year 2024; volatile markets and exchange rates expected in the near future

In fiscal year 2024, Burckhardt Compression continued on its growth path and delivered strong financials in volatile markets with diverse trends across segments and regions. Fabrice Billard, CEO of Burckhardt Compression, says: "The company delivered record financials, surpassing CHF 1 bn sales and CHF 100 mn net income for the first time. Both divisions contributed to these results. This reflects our strong positioning and ongoing success in markets aligned with global megatrends." The company benefits from a strong order backlog, a solid balance sheet and strong customer relationships. Despite current uncertainty related to the geopolitical landscape and tariffs, it expects fiscal year 2025 to be at a similar level as 2024.

Strong sales growth – Profitability increase in both divisions and at the Group level

Order intake for the Group reached CHF 1’151.2 mn, an increase of 2.4%, respectively 3.5% net of currency translation effects. Starting in fiscal year 2024, the accounting policy for the recognition of revenue for large projects has been changed from "Completed Contract Method" to "Percentage of Completion" (PoC) to better reflect the value creation process and to increase stability in revenue recognition*. To enable comparison with the year under review, prior year numbers are restated for PoC accounting. Sales were up by 12.6%, at CHF 1'095.6 mn, driven by an 18.2% growth in the Systems Division. Gross profit margin reached 28.0%, up 1.8pp year-on-year, due to a more favorable product mix in both divisions, the higher capacity utilization in all manufacturing and assembly facilities and the closure of low-profit service locations in the US. Research & Development expenses increased by 12.8% to CHF 30.1 mn, to support the development of new applications. Selling, marketing, and general administrative expenses amounted to 11.9% of sales, a reduction of 0.4pp compared to the previous year. This highlights the leverage and effectiveness of SG&A spend, which is part of the Mid-Range Plan. Other operating income and expenses amounted to CHF -5.6 mn (net), mainly consisting of negative FX effects and bad debt provisions (prior year: CHF 5.4 mn, including the recovery of bad debt provisions). The consolidated operating income (EBIT) recorded a substantial increase of 23.2% to CHF 140.8 mn. Both the Systems and Services Divisions increased their EBIT margins, by 2.7 and 0.1 percentage points respectively, resulting in an overall Group EBIT margin of 12.9%, up from 11.7% in the previous year.

Value creation further enhanced – Dividend of CHF 18.00 proposed

Financial expenses in line with the previous year and a similar tax rate of 23.2% (compared to 23.8% last year) resulted in a net income of CHF 105.6 mn, which exceeded the previous year’s figure by 25.0%*. Accordingly, earnings per share attributable to Burckhardt Compression Group shareholders rose from CHF 24.98 to CHF 31.20.

Value creation was further enhanced, with Return on Net Operating Assets (RONOA) increasing from 28.3% to 32.6%. Total equity increased to CHF 340.2 mn (CHF +43.8 mn), while the equity ratio increased to 29.1%. Based on these results, the Board of Directors will propose to the Annual General Meeting a dividend of CHF 18.00 per share, an increase of 16.1% compared with the previous year. This is within the Group’s overall attractive dividend policy of a 50% to 70% payout ratio.

Continued new equipment growth

Following a peak in 2022, the global Systems market further normalized in 2024, with a decrease predominantly driven by policy shifts in the Hydrogen Mobility and Energy segment. Despite this, the Systems Division’s leading position in growing sub-segments of the market resulted in a strong order intake of CHF 825.4 mn, representing a 5.8% increase over the previous year.

Marine applications like LPG, ammonia and LNG tankers have been growing at a good pace, driven by the increasing global energy trade as well as new regulations promoting sustainable shipping fuels. Linked to the expected further growth of solar panels and to geopolitics, the demand for Hyper Compressors used in the production of ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) remained high. Conversely, after rapid growth in the past years, the renewable hydrogen economy entered a period of recalibration. This shift is a direct response to higher-than-expected project costs, delays in finalizing regulations and subsidies, and the uncertainty created by elections in Europe and the US. Other new energy applications, like biogas and sustainable aviation fuels, continued to progress.

Focus on high-margin services in a market showing regional disparities

The Services markets have been strongly influenced by local economic conditions. The European market decreased due to economic and political uncertainty. On the positive side, Asia-Pacific and the regions of the Middle East, Central Asia, and Eastern Europe grew at a good pace, driven by the installed base and large upgrade projects. In the US, the market was stable with regional disparities, but order intake decreased due to the closure of three low-profit service centers, as announced in the first-half year report. On a global basis, the Services Division’s presence in the marine market is further increasing, benefiting from a growing installed base, a strong service network, and new offerings. Against this backdrop, order intake for the Services Division fell by 5.4% (-4.5% net of currency translation effects) to CHF 325.8 mn.

Further progress in the company's transformation

The fiscal year 2024 marks another successful step in the implementation of the company's Mid-Range Plan, which was communicated in November 2022, with an increase of the mid-range guidance in June 2024. It is based on four pillars: strengthening the core business, transforming and building new growth avenues, operational excellence, and further enhancing the business foundations. Burckhardt Compression made tangible progress across these pillars. For instance, with its focus on transforming and building new growth avenues, the company launched the new digital services UP! Insight and UP! Detect to help its customers optimize their compressor fleet’s reliability and uptime. With regards to operational excellence, the company has further leveraged its asset base in all factories to increase sales by more than 18% in the Systems Division without significant capital investments. To further enhance its business foundations, Burckhardt Compression reduced its greenhouse gas emission intensity (Scope 1 and 2) by 40.0%, a giant step forward on its path towards reaching net zero (Scope 1 and 2) in 2035.

Upcoming changes in the Board of Directors

Ton Büchner has decided to reduce his portfolio of mandates towards the end of 2025. As part of a planned and managed succession, Jacques Sanche will be proposed to the Annual General Meeting on July 5, 2025, as a new and additional member of the Board of Directors of Burckhardt Compression. The Board of Directors will additionally propose Jacques Sanche as its new Chair, effective December 15, 2025, following a transition period of over five months. At that time, Ton Büchner will step down from the Board of Directors, bringing the total number of Board Members back to six.

Jacques Sanche is a recognized industrial leader who currently holds the CEO position at Bucher Industries AG and has been the CEO of Belimo Holding AG. The 59-year-old Swiss-Canadian has announced his retirement from his current position as CEO for April 2026, when he will hand over to an internal successor. Ton Büchner states, "We are very pleased to propose Jacques Sanche to our Board of Directors, and I look forward to working with him during the transition phase. Having been a public CEO for 17 years, he will add considerable value". Jacques Sanche states: "I have admired the key role that Burckhardt Compression is playing as the world diversifies its energy sources. With its strategic focus on a more sustainable future, the company has a strong momentum. It would be an honor to support the great team at Burckhardt Compression by joining the Board of Directors".

Guidance for fiscal year 2025 – At a similar level as in fiscal year 2024

Burckhardt Compression has entered the fiscal year 2025 amid a dynamic operating environment characterized by uncertainties surrounding global trade tariffs and currency exchange rates. While these developments have led to downward revisions in global GDP growth forecasts, the company remains confident in its strategy and ability to navigate evolving market conditions. The robust order backlog, coupled with a solid balance sheet and strong customer relationships, provides stability. Assuming there is no further escalation in trade disputes and macroeconomic conditions remain relatively stable, the company expects:

  • Sales at around CHF 1.1 bn at the Group level
  • EBIT margin similar to fiscal year 2024
  • Stronger profitability in the second half due to the product and service mix

The Group will continue to actively monitor the macro environment and any potential impact it may have on the business.

Global megatrends underpin Mid-Range Plan trajectory

Beyond short-term uncertainties, Burckhardt Compression's strategy is supported by global megatrends. A growing global population, especially the middle class, creates increased demand for essential products like fertilizers and polymers and for investment in energy infrastructure. Ensuring a stable and secure energy supply in a rapidly evolving geopolitical landscape with growing intermittent energy sources requires significant investment in energy storage, gas pipelines and transportation infrastructure, e.g. for LNG or LPG. In addition, the energy transition increases the share of natural gas in the energy mix and requires significant investments in renewable energy infrastructure, which includes solar panels and low-carbon fuels. All these applications require compressors. With its increased R&D activities and the ability to develop innovative solutions in partnership with its customers, Burckhardt Compression stands at the forefront of these developments.

* The accounting policy for the recognition of revenue for projects above CHF 7 mn and lasting more than 1 year has been changed from "Completed Contract Method" to "Percentage of Completion" (PoC) to better reflect the value creation process and to increase stability in revenue recognition.

The annual report 2024 and further information on the fiscal year 2024 are available on the website on: www.burckhardtcompression.com/financial-reports.

Attachments

  • burckhardt-compression-continues-deliver-strong-growth-increased-profitability-fy24-DE
  • burckhardt-compression-continues-deliver-strong-growth-increased-profitability-fy24-EN
  • burckhardt-compression-continues-deliver-strong-growth-increased-profitability-fy24-FR
  • burckhardt-compression-continues-deliver-strong-growth-increased-profitability-fy24-IT

data.title: Shanghai Jin Jiang International Hotels Appoints Auditor for Proposed Hong Kong Listing
data.description:
Shanghai Jin Jiang International Hotels appointed KPMG as auditor for a planned H-share listing in Hong Kong.

KPMG will also serve as the hospitality company's overseas auditor for the first fiscal year post-listing, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The appointment is subject to shareholder approval.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.5% at 81,411, while the NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.5% to 24,744, mirroring gains in Asian peers.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was nearly flat versus the U.S. dollar at 85.8825, wedged between an uptick in Asian peers and dollar demand from local corporates, including oil companies.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 100.965 rupees, with the yield down slightly at 6.1973%, with traders focused on the RBI's monetary policy decision on Friday.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 basis points at 5.52%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined 4 bps to 5.59%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.80%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.67%.

data.title: Yaqeen Capital's Vice Chairman to Take Over Interim Chair Role
data.description:
Yaqeen Capital (SASE:9602) said Wednesday its Vice Chairman Salman bin Abdulaziz bin Shaheen will assume the chairman's duties until a successor is appointed.

The Saudi Arabian investment bank's Chairman Abdul Mohsen Mohammed Al-Saleh stepped down from the position, effective June 1.

data.title: Suzuki Motor halted Swift car production due to China's rare earth curb, Nikkei says
data.description:
Suzuki Motor halted production of its Swift model cars in Japan since May 26 due to China's rare earth restrictions, the Nikkei reported on Thursday.

The Japanese automaker had announced the planned production stoppage of the popular model through June 6 but had not given a reason.

Suzuki spokesperson was not immediate available for a comment when contacted by Reuters.

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on June 5
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 39.4350 against the U.S. dollar at 0435 GMT, compared with a close of 39.2340 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 2.13% higher at 9,474.06 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Shares in Asia crept higher and the U.S. dollar languished ahead of the European Central Bank offering its policy outlook for a tumultuous global economy.

CENBANK DATA (TRNFR=ECI)

The Central Bank will announce weekly money and banking statistics. (1130 GMT)

FLIGHTS TO DAMASCUS

Turkish Airlines subsidiary AJet said it will start flights to Damascus International Airport from Istanbul and Ankara airports in mid-June.

DEFENCE MINISTER

Turkey will press European allies which plan to sharply ramp up their defence spending to ease restrictions that now require most of that money to be spent in the EU, Defence Minister Yasar Guler told Reuters on Wednesday ahead of a NATO meeting.

Turkey is training and advising Syria's armed forces and helping improve its defences, and has no immediate plans for the withdrawal or relocation of its troops stationed there, Defence Minister Yasar Guler told Reuters.

NATO DEFENCE MINISTERS MEETING

Defence ministers from the alliance, including Turkey, meet in Brussels.

data.title: Fitch Rates ANZ's 1.5-Billion-Euro Mortgage Covered Bond Issuance
data.description:
Fitch Ratings assigned an "AAA" rating to ANZ Group Holdings' 1.5 billion euros of series 2025-1 fixed-rate mortgage covered bonds, with a stable outlook, according to a Thursday statement from the credit ratings agency.

The rating on the bank's long-term issuer default rating of "AA-," as well as the various uplifts above the issuer default rating granted to the program, and the overcollateralization protection provided through the program's asset percentage.

The ratings firm's analysis relies on the program's committed asset percentage used in the program's asset coverage test of about 91%, which provides more protection than Fitch's AAA breakeven asset percentage of 96%.

The stable outlook reflects a four-notch buffer against an issuer default rating downgrade, the report said.

data.title: Aussie little troubled by soft data, kiwi eyes 7-month top
data.description:
The Australian dollar was little troubled by soft data on Thursday as fears of a tariff-induced slowdown in the U.S. lifted Treasuries and dragged down the greenback, while the kiwi was perched within a whisker of a seven-month high.

The Aussie was flat at $0.6493, having risen 0.5% overnight as the U.S. dollar slid, offsetting the impact from a weak GDP report on Wednesday. It was, however, still stuck within the recent range of $0.6360 and $0.6537.

The kiwi dollar inched up 0.1% on Thursday to $0.6028. It rallied 0.5% overnight and is now just a touch below the seven-month peak of $0.6054 hit earlier this week.

Data released on Wednesday showed the U.S. services sector contracted for the first time in nearly a year, while private payrolls increased far less than expected in May. Treasuries rallied as investors added to their bets of a U.S. rate cut in September, while the greenback fell to six-week lows.

Down Under, a slew of soft economic data this week had traders wagering the Reserve Bank of Australia will ease again as early as July, and they see rates bottoming around 2.85% by early next year. (0#AUDIRPR)

On Thursday, figures showed Australian household spending rose only marginally in April, a further sign consumption was still tepid despite a drop in borrowing rates and cooling inflation.

"The momentum of the HSI (Household Spending Index) in recent months continues to fade, and is also broadly consistent with retail sales," said George Tharenou, an economist at UBS. "The risks of a July cut have increased materially."

Other data showed Australia's surplus on traded goods narrowed in April as exports of gold pulled back from record highs. Exports to China remained resilient.

Across the Tasman Sea, with the Reserve Bank of New Zealand having already cut a hefty 225 bps this cycle to the neutral zone, markets are betting it is most likely to deliver one last rate cut in August. (0#NZDIRPR)

data.title: SBM Offshore to Exit Equatorial Guinea with Aseng Vessel Sale
data.description:
SBM Offshore said Wednesday it agreed to sell its equity interest in the lease and operating entities of the Aseng floating production storage and offloading vessel to GEPetrol, the national oil company of Equatorial Guinea.

The exit from Equatorial Guinea will occur after a transition period of up to 12 months. The move aligns with the offshore energy company's ongoing strategy to streamline its Lease and Operate portfolio.

The deal is subject to standard conditions and regulatory approvals.

data.title: Silver Holds Near 7-Month Highs on Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
Silver prices hovered around $34.50 per ounce on Thursday, near their highest levels in seven months, as a wave of disappointing US economic data weighed on the dollar and boosted demand for safe-haven assets.

The ADP report released Wednesday showed the US private sector added just 37,000 jobs in May—well below expectations and the weakest reading in over two years.

Additionally, the ISM services PMI signaled contraction for the first time in nearly a year, with a sharp drop in new business and rising input costs, likely linked to new tariffs.

President Donald Trump continued to pressure the Federal Reserve to cut rates, but officials have so far favored a cautious approach amid heightened uncertainty.

On the trade front, Trump further dampened sentiment by saying it was “extremely hard” to reach a deal with Chinese President Xi Jinping, despite earlier indications that the two leaders may speak this week.

data.title: Market Chatter: Airbus' Chief Procurement Officer to Lead India Operations
data.description:
Airbus' Chief Procurement Officer Juergen Westermeier was reportedly appointed to lead the company's operations in India and South Asia, effective Sept. 1.

The French aerospace company did not name his successor, saying the matter is "subject to further notice," Reuters wrote Wednesday, citing an internal memo.

Westermeier succeeds Remi Maillard, who was appointed as the company's head of technology, according to the news outlet.

A source close to the company told the publication that Westermeier's move is not connected to recent supply chain issues.

Airbus did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.
